evals.name: security-secrets-scan
evals.prompt: Run the tenet-security and tenet-secrets skills against this codebase. Scan for injection risks, hardcoded secrets, auth gaps, and CORS issues. Write findings to .healthcheck/reports/security.json and .healthcheck/reports/secrets.json following the Tenet report schema.
evals.expected_output: Two JSON files with scored findings — security should flag any eval/exec usage, SQL injection risks, CORS config; secrets should detect any hardcoded keys or .env patterns. Both must include fix_prompts for every finding.

evals.name: complexity-solid-analysis
evals.prompt: Run the tenet-complexity and tenet-solid skills against this codebase. Measure cyclomatic complexity, function length, file length, and nesting depth. Then evaluate SOLID principle adherence — look for SRP violations, concrete dependencies in business logic, and fat interfaces. Write findings to .healthcheck/reports/complexity.json and .healthcheck/reports/solid.json.
evals.expected_output: Two JSON files. Complexity should flag functions with cyclomatic complexity >10, files >500 lines, functions >50 lines. SOLID should identify god classes or modules with mixed responsibilities. Both must use the scoring formula and include fix_prompts.

evals.name: testing-debt-docs
evals.prompt: Run the tenet-testing, tenet-debt, and tenet-docs skills against this codebase. Evaluate test coverage and quality (look for .only, skipped tests, coverage reports). Scan for aged TODO/FIXME/HACK comments and commented-out code. Check README completeness and inline doc coverage. Write findings to .healthcheck/reports/testing.json, .healthcheck/reports/debt.json, and .healthcheck/reports/docs.json.
evals.expected_output: Three JSON files. Testing should find the e2e test structure and flag any .only or skipped tests. Debt should find and age TODO/FIXME comments via git blame. Docs should evaluate README sections. All must include fix_prompts and use the scoring formula.

evals.name: errors-observability-buildci
evals.prompt: Run the tenet-errors, tenet-observability, and tenet-build-ci skills against this codebase. Check for empty catch blocks, unhandled promise rejections, and missing error boundaries. Evaluate logging patterns, health endpoints, and graceful shutdown. Audit the CI config in .github/workflows/ci.yml for lockfile presence, test steps, and pinned versions. Write findings to .healthcheck/reports/errors.json, .healthcheck/reports/observability.json, and .healthcheck/reports/build-ci.json.
evals.expected_output: Three JSON files. Errors should flag empty catches and broad exception handling. Observability should check for structured logging and health endpoints. Build-CI should audit the GitHub Actions workflow. All must include fix_prompts.

evals.name: performance-deps-api-a11y
evals.prompt: Run the tenet-performance, tenet-dependencies, tenet-api-contract, and tenet-accessibility skills against this codebase. Look for N+1 patterns, memory leaks, and React re-render hazards. Check package.json for outdated or vulnerable dependencies. Evaluate API route consistency if API routes are found. Scan TSX files for missing alt text, ARIA labels, and form label associations. Write findings to .healthcheck/reports/performance.json, .healthcheck/reports/dependencies.json, .healthcheck/reports/api-contract.json, and .healthcheck/reports/accessibility.json. If a dimension is not applicable, set score to null and applicable to false.
evals.expected_output: Four JSON files. Performance should find React hazards in the web app and any N+1 patterns in the API. Dependencies should check package.json health. API contract should evaluate route handlers if present. Accessibility should scan TSX for a11y issues. Each must use the scoring formula and include fix_prompts.

evals.name: ops-privacy-supplychain-infra-db-release
evals.prompt: Run the tenet-privacy-data, tenet-supply-chain-license, tenet-infra-cloud, tenet-database-migrations, and tenet-release-ops skills against this codebase. Check PII handling and retention, SBOM/license/provenance gaps, infrastructure-as-code exposure, migration safety, and release/rollback readiness. Write findings to .healthcheck/reports/privacy-data.json, .healthcheck/reports/supply-chain-license.json, .healthcheck/reports/infra-cloud.json, .healthcheck/reports/database-migrations.json, and .healthcheck/reports/release-ops.json. If a dimension is not applicable, set score to null and applicable to false.
evals.expected_output: Five JSON files. Applicable dimensions should include scored findings with fix_prompts and confidence tiers; non-applicable dimensions should set score to null and applicable to false. All findings must follow the Tenet location contract for file/line and fix_prompt Location.

evals.name: correctness-logic-bugs
evals.prompt: Run the tenet-correctness skill against this codebase. Select a bounded review set from critical paths (auth, payments, data mutation), churn hotspots, and entry-point handlers, then read each unit and trace concrete inputs looking for logic bugs: off-by-one, inverted conditions, wrong-variable copy-paste, missing await, race conditions, null paths, and type-coercion errors. State a concrete failing input for every major/critical finding. Write findings to .healthcheck/reports/correctness.json following the Tenet report schema.
evals.expected_output: One JSON file. correctness.json must select a bounded review set (not the whole repo) and state which files were and were not reviewed in notes. Every major/critical finding must include a concrete 'input X -> wrong output Y' scenario, a snippet, and a fix_prompt. Must use the scoring formula and honor suppressions.

evals.name: security-fixture-recall
evals.prompt: Run the tenet-security skill against the evals/fixtures/security-recall/ directory as the project root. Build the entry-point inventory, scan every vulnerability class, and run the mandatory verification pass. Write findings to .healthcheck/reports/security.json. Then score with: python3 evals/score-fixtures.py --expected evals/fixtures/security-recall/EXPECTED.json --report .healthcheck/reports/security.json
evals.expected_output: security.json plus a passing score run: recall >= 80% of the 20 planted vulnerabilities across all classes (SQL/command/XSS/NoSQL injection, IDOR, tenant isolation, mass assignment, path traversal, JWT alg-none, timing-unsafe compare, weak hashing, weak random, SSRF, CORS, unverified webhook, prototype pollution, zip-slip, default creds, unsafe yaml.load, mobile insecure storage) AND zero false positives on the 9 safe decoys (parameterized query, Drizzle sql tagged template, DOMPurify-wrapped HTML, execFile array, Math.random jitter, sha1 cache key, ownership-scoped query, publishable Stripe key, allowlisted SSRF).
